title: Issue No. 74
preview: Today: Saie Beauty is quietly axing half its blush shade range while calling it a "seasonal refresh," the adenosine molecule your Japanese sunscreen has been hiding in plain sight, Schiaparelli's AW26 show where every model looked like a gilded wound, and the nonprofit putting dermatology care in the hands of incarcerated women who haven't seen a doctor in years. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 73
preview: Today: Tower 28 is quietly reformulating its SOS Spray and praying the eczema community doesn't notice, the ectoin molecule that extremophile bacteria figured out before any of us, Rick Owens's AW26 show where every model's lips looked vacuum-sealed in vaseline, and the nonprofit handing out SPF to outdoor workers who've never owned a bottle of sunscreen. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 72
preview: Today: Merit Beauty is quietly shrinking its complexion range while calling it a "simplification," the bakuchiol derivative that just upstaged retinol in a head-to-head clinical trial, Valentino's AW26 show where every model looked like they'd been kissed by a ghost, and the nonprofit teaching women in domestic violence shelters how to reclaim their own reflection. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 71
preview: Today: Ilia Beauty is quietly discontinuing shades across its serum skin tint line and hoping nobody does the math, the saccharide isomerate that makes your skin think it's hydrated from the inside, Bottega Veneta's AW26 show where every model looked like they'd just come in from weeping in the rain, and the nonprofit providing free skincare to unhoused women in Los Angeles. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 70
preview: Today: Kosas is quietly pulling its bestselling concealer from international markets and nobody's talking about why, the tranexamic acid derivative that pigmentation researchers are actually excited about, Miu Miu's AW26 show where every model wore what looked like a fresh crying jag, and the nonprofit bringing free dermatology visits to migrant farmworkers. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 68
preview: Today: Rare Beauty's quiet pricing shakeup has Sephora employees whispering, the snail mucin alternative that actual dermatologists are switching to, Acne Studios AW26 and the unsettling beauty of a completely bare face in a snowstorm, and the charity teaching trans women how to do their own makeup for the first time. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 67
preview: Today: Aesop's parent company is quietly gutting the brand's cult formulations, the fermented rice filtrate that Korean grandmothers have been right about all along, Prada's AW26 show where every model's skin looked almost uncomfortably bare, and the charity teaching burn survivors how to use color cosmetics as a form of reclamation. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 66
preview: Today: Byredo's strange pivot into skincare has the fragrance world uneasy, the ceramide precursor most people are confusing with the real thing, Simone Rocha's AW26 show that turned bruised knees into a beauty statement, and the tiny nonprofit giving free facials to hospice patients. Let's get into it.

title: Issue No. 65
preview: Today: Glossier's quiet reformulation scandal, the copper peptide moment that won't quit, Dries Van Noten's final bow on the SS26 runway, and a charity putting lipstick in the hands of women rebuilding their lives. Pull up a chair.
